rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Settlement Of Account And Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs For 2018 And 2019
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The Petition for Settlement of Account and Approval of Attorney Fees and Reimbursement of Costs for 2018 and 2019 issues: a. On Schedule C: Disbursements 7/16/2018 $200.00 withdrawal Dessie Silva is not explained. b. On Schedule C: Disbursements 3/5/2019 $7,781.24 Brian McCaffrey attorney fees is not explained. c. On Schedule C: Disbursements 3/18/2019 withdrawal of $2,000.00 is not explained. d. On Schedule C: Disbursements 4/9/2019 transfer of $2,000.00 is not explained. e. Attorney requests $10,010.00 in attorney fees and $504.74 in costs for 2018 be approved and $7,781.25 from trust funds and the successor f. Attorney requests $30,270.50 in fees and $1,939.38 in costs for 2019 be approved and successor trustee be authorized to pay. g. It appears the attorney spent approximately 50.7 hours @ $385.00 per hour on the accounts which appears extraordinarily high based on the simplicity of the accounts. h. Attorney is billing 4 hours @ his attorney rate of $385.00 per hour to travel to get signatures.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The [second] supplement, filed on 10/1/21, was not signed by the other co-executor, Robert Noelke. [CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Section 1023.] This has been pointed out in connection with other papers filed with the Court and signed by just one of the two co-executors. 2) The "statements" attached to the [second] supplement filed on 10/1/21 do not all clearly identify the financial institution and are not original statements. Furthermore, the totals shown on those statements or printouts are far less than the cash that was reported to be on hand at the end of either this amended first account or even of the amended second account (which has not yet been fully reviewed), which may need clarification. 3) The proof of payment to the IRS was just filed on 10/29/21, not at least one week before this hearing, as the Court directed on 10/6/21 and shows that the amount due by 12/17/20 was paid. It is not clear if that is the current amount due to the IRS. 4) Co-executor Patricia Costello merely states that the explanation regarding the reverse mortgage was cited in the petition for letters of special administration, without submitting any documents supporting the payment of $1,131,938.00 to Monarda Investments LLC on 10/13/17. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The proof of service shows notice of the amended petition was given before the amended petition was filed. LRSF 14.14A ("Notice may not be mailed, served, or published before the filing of the pleading requiring notice."). 2) The Court is to review the terms of the Settlement Agreement with its modified Addendum. The proposed order includes language requiring the parties to comply with the settlement terms, but the amended petition only requests approval of the settlement terms. rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/2021 1) Please explain the legal basis / cite probate code sections that allow expenses that have not occurred to be reimbursed to the attorney. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RESTATEMENT OF THE RAYMOND J. SCHWEIZER 1939 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Settlement Of First Account And Approval Of Trustee And Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges that venue is proper here as the principal place of administration is at the Trustee's attorney's office located in San Francisco. 2) Trustee received $96,971 in fees for the accounting period based upon arrangement with Settlor during his lifetime, as adjusted on 6/1/08, after notification to the current beneficiaries. Petitioner alleges that timesheets were not kept as the trust was not under court supervision and fees were based upon that agreement. 3) Prior attorney for trustee, Morrison & Forester, was paid $98,320 in fees for their services for the accounting period. Petitioner states there was an overpayment of $42,658.48 which was credited in future invoices, and which will be reflected in future accountings. 4) Many of the entries in attorney's timesheets are redacted. 5) Petitioner requests authorization to pay any balance of fees from principal if income is insufficient. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDDA CABRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/2021 1) There is no publication on file for the amended petition. (See CRC 7.53 and Probate Code 8121.) 2) Petitioner has not filed or given notice on DE-121. (Probate Code 8100) 3) Petitioner did not check required boxes on the petition page 3 #5a. (7) or (8). 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Examiner received unfiled courtesy copies that have been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/29/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TIMOTHY ANDREW LESHER IRREVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee On Consent Of Beneficiary Of Timothy Andrew Lesher Irrevocable Tr
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: How is venue proper when the resigning successor trustee is in Los Angeles? This trust was already brought before this Court in case PTR-19-302789 (when the then-resigning trustee was in San Francisco). If this is the proper venue, this petition should have been filed in, or should now be consolidated with, the earlier case. No courtesy copy of the proof of service has been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/19/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID T. CHIANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 12/13/2018 that was previously admitted on 3/19/2021 by Bergen County Surrogate's Court in New Jersey. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set on 11/21/22. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 11/21/2022. Appearance will be required on 11/21/2022 if no petition has been filed.
